seeds.title: Erdos q(6): minimum edges in a 6-uniform intersecting hypergraph with cover number 6
seeds.query: q(6) minimum edges 6-uniform pairwise-intersecting hypergraph cover number six exact witness verifier 5-cover rejection orderly generation canonical augmentation tau-deficiency prune bracket 14 18
seeds.falsifier: A proposed witness has two disjoint edges or admits a hitting set of size at most 5; an optimality claim lacks a complete independently checkable nonexistence certificate.
seeds.verifier: Given an edge list of 6-sets, check every edge pair intersects and exhaustively reject every hitting set of size at most 5. Any edge gives cover number at most 6, so no 5-cover certifies cover number exactly 6. Optimality still requires a complete nonexistence certificate for every smaller edge count.
seeds.next_action: Replay the exact witness verifier after its final write and record its SHA-256, with known-good and known-bad fixtures whose assertions check the intended rejection reason, not only the final valid/invalid boolean. Then execute one bounded orderly-generation/canonical-augmentation step with a tau-deficiency prune; do not repeat the failed SAT/DRAT encoding and do not claim optimality without complete exhaustion.
seeds.avoid: Do not repeat the failed SAT/DRAT route: D(5,12) and D(6,14) already timed out with global tau-constraint explosion. Do not launch broad search or claim q(6); use orderly generation/canonical augmentation with tau-deficiency pruning only after exact verifier and kill fixtures. No Atlas writes or external submissions.
